title: lefebvrea abyssinica
intro: Lefebvrea abyssinica, also known as Ethiopian toothache plant, is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae, native to Africa. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which have been traditionally used to treat toothaches, headaches, and other pains. The health benefits of Lefebvrea abyssinica include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and potentially treating various other conditions due to its antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Lefebvrea abyssinica can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by applying the plant's essential oils topically to affected areas.
